agency: DEPT OF DEFENSE · DEFENSE LOGISTICS AGENCY
title: HOUSING ASSY,ACTUAT NSN:1680-01-137-3534 P/N:70410-02501-045
bid_recommendation: without the TDP, responsible pricing of a UH-60 actuator housing assembly on an LPTA/initial-offer basis is impossible, and the June 25 deadline leaves fewer than 21 calendar days to resolve certification gaps, obtain and analyze the technical data package, build a compliant cost breakdown, and confirm approved-source traceability.
overview_summary: DLA Aviation Huntsville is soliciting quotations for 8 each Housing Assembly Actuator (NSN: 1680-01-137-3534, P/N: 70410-02501-045) in support of the UH-60 Blackhawk weapon system. This is a 100% small business set-aside, one-time buy, firm fixed price acquisition. Deliveries are required to commence 135 days after contract award to the Corpus Christi Army Depot.

overview_json.scope: Manufacture and deliver 8 each Housing Assembly, Actuator (Noun: HOUSING ASSY,ACTUAT; NSN: 1680-01-137-3534; P/N: 70410-02501-045; Mfr CAGE: 81996) in support of the UH-60 (BE) Blackhawk weapon system. Packaging IAW MIL-STD-2073-1 with military-level preservation, Level B packing, and contractor-furnished reusable container. Inspection and acceptance at origin. Arms Export Control Act applies; JCP certification required to access TDP.
overview_json.summary: DLA Aviation Huntsville is soliciting quotations for 8 each Housing Assembly Actuator (NSN: 1680-01-137-3534, P/N: 70410-02501-045) in support of the UH-60 Blackhawk weapon system. This is a 100% small business set-aside, one-time buy, firm fixed price acquisition. Deliveries are required to commence 135 days after contract award to the Corpus Christi Army Depot.
overview_json.customer: Defense Logistics Agency (DLA) Aviation Huntsville, DLA/AHAA, Redstone Arsenal AL 35898-7340
overview_json.eval_basis: Award will be made using Lowest Priced Technically Acceptable (LPTA) procedures per FAR 15.101-2. The Government will award to the offeror submitting a technically acceptable proposal with the lowest overall price.
overview_json.primary_objective: Delivery of 8 each UH-60 actuator housing assemblies to SR W0MU USA DEP Corpus Christi, 540 First St SE Bldg 1846, Corpus Christi TX 78419-5255, commencing 135 days after contract award.

submission_requirements.requirement: Submit proposal via email only — fax not accepted

Provide cost/pricing data breakdown (all cost elements including profit/fee) or sales history if requested per FAR 15.403-3

risks_summary: Severity 8/10 · 7 prioritized · top: Per A-11, both the offeror AND any source of supply must hold an active United S

price_risks: Container price must appear as a discrete line item within the CLIN 0001AA pricing section per A-9 — missing this breakout is a technical unacceptability ground under LPTA.
LPTA with possible award on initial offers means no reverse auction repricing safety net — first price submitted may be final.
Cost-element breakdown including profit/fee must be producible on demand under A-23/FAR 15.403-3 or the proposal is declared non-responsive.
top_3_risks: JCP certification must be active for both offeror and manufacturer before TDP access is granted — DLA will not delay award for an ineligible offeror, making this an instant disqualifier if not verified immediately.

TDP is not attached to the SAM notice and must be requested by email with PRON 'DOA1'; the DoD SAFE link expires in 7 calendar days, so a delayed request against a June 25 deadline means you cannot price the CLIN at all.

CAGE 81996 manufacturer approval is embedded in CLIN 0001AA and QAR will reject items not produced by the approved manufacturer — if your firm is a non-manufacturer, source confirmation and the 500-employee nonmanufacturer rule compliance must be documented before submission.
schedule_risks: 135-day delivery commencement is fixed from contract award date with FOB Origin terms; DPAS DO-rated order requires 15-day written notification if schedule cannot be met, and silence constitutes acceptance.
7-calendar-day DoD SAFE expiration window for TDP download creates a hard upstream scheduling constraint — late TDP receipt compresses your should-cost and pricing development timeline.
technical_risks: CAGE 81996 approved-manufacturer traceability required at origin inspection — non-manufacturer must furnish objective evidence per Inspection & Acceptance narrative.
ANSI/ISO/ASQ 9001:2015 or AS9100 QMS compliance (with 7.3 tailoring) required under FAR 52.246-11; absent certification is a DCMA audit trigger.
MIL-STD-2073-1 Level B preservation and MIL-STD-129 bar code markings required per packaging narrative — contractor must have or source compliant packaging capability before delivery commences.

dfars_trap_risks.trap_name: NIST SP 800-171 DoD Assessment — SPRS score required
dfars_trap_risks.specific_risk: Award is blocked if your basic assessment score is absent or stale in SPRS; DLA's automated pre-award check will flag a missing score before the KO can make award.
dfars_trap_risks.consequence_if_missed: Proposal is technically unacceptable and ineligible for award regardless of price.
dfars_trap_risks.required_verification: Log into SPRS (https://www.sprs.csd.disa.mil/) and confirm a current 252.204-7020 Basic self-assessment is posted with a date within 3 years.

dfars_trap_risks.trap_name: Safeguarding Covered Defense Information — cyber incident reporting
dfars_trap_risks.specific_risk: Clause 45 (DFARS 252.204-7012 Deviation 2024-O0013 Rev 1) requires adequate security per NIST SP 800-171 and mandatory 72-hour cyber incident reporting; a small business without a System Security Plan and documented implementation may be found non-compliant during DCMA review.
dfars_trap_risks.consequence_if_missed: Potential cure notice, contract termination for default, and debarment risk for repeated non-compliance.
dfars_trap_risks.required_verification: Confirm your firm has a documented SSP covering NIST SP 800-171 controls and a written cyber incident response procedure before award.

dfars_trap_risks.trap_name: Export-Controlled Items — Arms Export Control Act applies
dfars_trap_risks.specific_risk: A-11 invokes the Arms Export Control Act (22 U.S.C. 2751 et seq.) and Executive Order 12470; sharing TDP with any non-JCP-certified party including foreign-national employees without a TAA or export license is a federal criminal violation.
dfars_trap_risks.consequence_if_missed: Severe criminal penalties under ITAR; contract termination; debarment.
dfars_trap_risks.required_verification: Confirm all personnel who will access the TDP are U.S. citizens or hold valid export authorization; do not share TDP with uncertified subcontractors.

evaluation_risks: LPTA basis of award with stated intent to award on initial proposals eliminates any expectation of discussions or price correction after submission.
Technically Unacceptable rating — triggered by missing CAGE code, incomplete reps/certs, absent container price breakout, or non-compliant QMS — results in immediate ineligibility with no cure.

prioritized_risks.text: Per A-11, both the offeror AND any source of supply must hold an active United States/Canada Joint Certification Program (JCP) certification to access the Arms Export Control Act-restricted TDP via DoD SAFE; DLA explicitly states it will NOT delay award for ineligible offerors to obtain JCP, meaning a missing or lapsed JCP results in permanent disqualification before pricing begins.
prioritized_risks.title: JCP cert gap blocks export-controlled TDP access
prioritized_risks.faraudit_action: Verify active JCP status for your firm AND your manufacturer this week at https://www.dla.mil/HQ/Logistics/LogisticsPlans/JCP/; email josh.long@dla.mil with your PRON (DOA1) to request TDP within the 7-calendar-day DoD SAFE window before the June 25 deadline.

prioritized_risks.text: Per A-11, DLA cannot attach the TDP to the SAM notice due to software constraints, and offerors must affirmatively request the package by email using PRON 'DOA1' in the subject line; the DoD SAFE link expires after 7 calendar days, so a late request against a June 25 response deadline risks receiving zero technical data before bid close.
prioritized_risks.title: TDP required to price
prioritized_risks.faraudit_action: Email josh.long@dla.mil TODAY with subject line 'DOA1 — TDP Request SPRRA1-26-Q-0034' and download the package within 7 calendar days of receipt; calendar a hard reminder or the SAFE link will expire and you cannot price the CLIN.

prioritized_risks.text: CLIN 0001AA specifies Mfr CAGE 81996 (PN 70410-02501-045) for the UH-60 Blackhawk Housing Assembly; if your firm is not CAGE 81996, you are a non-manufacturer and must demonstrate the item was 'produced by the approved manufacturer' per the QAR Inspection and Acceptance clause (page 6), while also satisfying the FAR 52.219-1 non-manufacturer rule 500-employee size cap under NAICS 336413 — failure to confirm approved-source status triggers rejection at origin inspection.
prioritized_risks.title: CAGE 81996 manufacturer approval
prioritized_risks.faraudit_action: Confirm your supplier is CAGE 81996 or a government-approved alternate source before submitting; include the manufacturer's CAGE code in the proposal per A-21, and verify your employee count is under 500 to satisfy the nonmanufacturer rule for this 100% SB set-aside.

prioritized_risks.text: CAGE 81996 manufacturer approval is embedded in CLIN 0001AA and QAR will reject items not produced by the approved manufacturer — if your firm is a non-manufacturer, source confirmation and the 500-employee nonmanufacturer rule compliance must be documented before submission.
prioritized_risks.title: CAGE 81996 manufacturer approval is embedded in CLIN

prioritized_risks.text: Section M states award will be Lowest Priced Technically Acceptable (LPTA) and 'the Government intends to make an award based on the initial proposals,' meaning DLA may award without discussions; the reverse auction clause L02 provides only a contingent alternative, so an uncompetitive first price or a technically unacceptable initial submission results in immediate elimination with no cure opportunity.
prioritized_risks.title: LPTA on initial offers
prioritized_risks.citation: Section M (LPTA narrative) / L02 Reverse Auction (Oct 2016)
prioritized_risks.faraudit_action: Obtain TDP immediately and build a fully-burdened should-cost before submitting; do not plan on a reverse auction round — treat your first submission as your last and verify all T&Cs, reps/certs, and CAGE code are complete to avoid a technical 'Unacceptable' rating.

prioritized_risks.text: Per CLIN 0001AA narrative (D001) and A-9, the reusable container (contractor-furnished) price MUST be incorporated into the unit price AND separately broken out within the CLIN section; failure to provide both the unit price line and a discrete container price line renders the CLIN pricing non-compliant and the proposal technically unacceptable under the LPTA evaluation framework.
prioritized_risks.title: Container price breakout mandatory or CLIN is non-responsive
prioritized_risks.faraudit_action: Structure your CLIN 0001AA pricing with three explicit line items: (1) unit price inclusive of container, (2) unit price ex-container, (3) container price — confirm formatting matches the CLIN section fields on page 5 before submitting via email per A-18.

prioritized_risks.text: FAR 52.204-7 (clause 92) and FAR 52.204-13 (clause 12) require active SAM registration at time of offer and maintenance through contract performance; DFARS 252.204-7007 (clause 88) requires annual reps/certs updated within the last 12 months, including the NAICS 336413 size standard — an expired or administratively locked SAM record results in disqualification under the LPTA framework.
prioritized_risks.title: SAM.gov registration and reps/certs must be current at
prioritized_risks.faraudit_action: Log into SAM.gov today and verify: (1) entity registration is active with no pending renewal, (2) reps/certs updated within 12 months, (3) NAICS 336413 size standard correctly reflected, and (4) electronic business POC is designated for WAWF per 252.232-7006.

risks_json.executive_risk_summary:
DLA Aviation Huntsville is buying 8 units of a UH-60 Blackhawk actuator housing assembly (NSN 1680-01-137-3534, PN 70410-02501-045, Mfr CAGE 81996) under a one-time-buy, firm-fixed-price, 100% small business set-aside with a June 25, 2026 response deadline and a 135-day delivery requirement to Corpus Christi, TX. The contract is LPTA with stated intent to award on initial proposals.

Three risks could end the bid before a single dollar is quoted. First, the JCP/ITAR barrier: both your firm and your manufacturer must hold an active JCP certification to receive the TDP — DLA will not delay award, so a lapsed cert means permanent disqualification. Second, the TDP is not attached to the SAM notice and must be actively requested by email; the DoD SAFE download link expires in 7 calendar days, so a delayed request against a 21-day window leaves insufficient time to build a defensible price. Third, the approved-manufacturer rule embedded in CLIN 0001AA means QAR will reject delivery of any item not traceable to CAGE 81996 at origin inspection — non-manufacturers must document source approval or face rejection with no recourse under FOB Origin terms.

Immediate actions ranked by urgency: (1) This week — verify JCP status at dla.mil/JCP for your firm and CAGE 81996 supplier; email josh.long@dla.mil with 'DOA1' in the subject line to request the TDP and download within 7 days. (2) By June 16 — confirm CAGE 81996 supplier approval, validate SPRS score is posted under DFARS 252.204-7020, and verify SAM.gov registration is active with current reps/certs under NAICS 336413. (3) By June 22 — complete should-cost build from TDP with full cost-element breakdown (direct materials, labor, overhead, G&A, profit) and structure CLIN 0001AA pricing with mandatory separate container price line per A-9. (4) By June 25 — submit via email only per A-18 with manufacturer CAGE code, all reps/certs, and 'SOURCE SELECTION INFORMATION SEE FAR 2.101 AND FAR 3.104' legend on all documents.
risks_json.bid_no_bid_recommendation: JCP certification status for both your firm and CAGE 81996 manufacturer must be confirmed active before you can even access the TDP — without the TDP, responsible pricing of a UH-60 actuator housing assembly on an LPTA/initial-offer basis is impossible, and the June 25 deadline leaves fewer than 21 calendar days to resolve certification gaps, obtain and analyze the technical data package, build a compliant cost breakdown, and confirm approved-source traceability.

document_type_rationale: The document header explicitly states 'REQUEST FOR QUOTATION' on Standard Form 18, and the SAM.gov metadata shows solicitationNumber 'SPRRA126Q0034' with a 'Q' designator indicating quotation. The document uses simplified acquisition procedures under FAR 13 with quotations noted as 'not offers.'
